threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You’re still using that satnav to get to the local shops instead of just following the sound of the ice cream van.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and watched a guy offer to trade his pizza for someone’s spare change. The pizza was cold, but he was serious.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was in Neukölln when the guy in front of me at the Kebab stand started arguing passionately with the vendor about the best kind of sauce, and I realized it was a normal Tuesday.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find Döner stands open at 4 AM, serving perfectly crispy potatoes as a side—nothing else hits post-party cravings quite like it.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's local when they instinctively check BART delays before planning a taco run, like it’s a spiritual rite.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus that stopped right at the turn for a taco truck. Half the riders hopped off for a quick bite. Talk about multitasking in a city that never stops!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals drink beer in bars; most of us sip on vermut at home, especially on Sundays. There's a whole ritual we're missing out on.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at a local 'siesta' when everything shuts down in the afternoon—especially restaurants.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 243 bus is reliable—nobody who’s been here long would trust it for their morning commute.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with a saxophone. Not a single person complained. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was home when I saw a parade of dogs in tutus heading to the canal for their Saturday brunch. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, the Kottbusser Tor at 3 AM feels like a carnival of street food and laughter, a bizarre blend of midnight chaos you won't find anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their go-to taco spot—if they mention Taqueria La Cumbre, they're probably just here for the job.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy juggling on the 14 at 16th. He dropped a ball, it rolled under the seats, and everyone just pretended it wasn’t happening.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for Gaudí snapshots, but they miss the real charm in the small textile shops hiding in the alleys.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they stand in the middle of Plaça del Rei, map in hand, looking utterly lost while locals just breeze by.
